planned_spool.also_planned_if_any_filled_comment: A spool missing Planned Start is normally Unplanned. But if it has already been Packed and/or Dispatched, it clearly WAS worked and completed - it's just missing from the planning sheet (e.g. added outside the normal planning cycle). Counting it as Unplanned would misrepresent real backlog, so any spool with a value in one of these fields is treated as Planned too, even without a Planned Start date.

line_history_override.comment: When a spool's Composite Key is found in the uploaded Line History Sheet with at least one non-blank Joint No. row: if any of that spool's Weld FitUp Date (AG) values is blank, stage is Fit-Up; else if any Welding FRun Date (AL) value is blank, stage is Welding; else stage is PDQC. The last entry in override_stage_order (PDQC) is where this override stops - PDQC/RFP/PDI/Packing/Dispatch are still decided by the normal date-field-based logic below. If the Composite Key isn't found in the sheet, or has no non-blank Joint No. rows, the existing date-field-based logic is used unchanged for that spool.

completed.also_completed_if_any_filled_comment: A spool is normally Completed once Packing has a date. But some spools are already Packed/Dispatched in reality and just missing a Packing entry on the DPR (e.g. entered straight to Dispatch) - especially spools that were never in the planning sheet to begin with. Counting only Packing here would understate the real Completed total, so any spool with a value in one of these fields is treated as Completed too, even without a Packing date.
